notes.completion_rate: 66% completion is below healthy threshold (80%+). Sprint was likely overcommitted or faced blocking issues. Review capacity planning and dependencies for PE Sprint 39.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint additions shows excellent sprint discipline and planning fidelity.
notes.rollover: No rollover items — clean slate for next sprint.
notes.cycle_time: 17d median cycle time is high and signals bottlenecks in workflow. Items are sitting in-progress too long; investigate blockers, handoffs, or PR review delays.
notes.type_split: 44% bugs exceeded healthy threshold (40%). High defect volume may indicate quality debt or recent production issues requiring remediation focus.

notes.completion_rate: 46% completion is below healthy threshold. High volume (325 items) with significant rollover suggests overcommitment or scope creep impacting delivery.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-scope additions shows strong sprint discipline and planning adherence.
notes.rollover: 192 items (59%) rolled over is concerning. Review sizing practices and capacity planning to reduce carryover in future sprints.
notes.cycle_time: 21d median cycle time is high and signals bottlenecks in workflow. Investigate blockers, review processes, or WIP limits to improve throughput.
notes.type_split: 32% bug ratio is healthy. 7 releases in quarter shows strong, consistent delivery cadence for mobile app with app store review constraints.

notes.completion_rate: 15% completion is critically low. Sprint closed with 160 items incomplete — indicates severe overcommitment or scope/planning breakdown.
notes.oos_total: Zero OOS shows disciplined scope control, but doesn't offset the planning failure evidenced by 15% completion.
notes.rollover: 183 items rolled over (97% of sprint) — massive carryover from prior sprint(s). Root cause analysis needed on why scope wasn't right-sized.
notes.cycle_time: 65d median cycle time is severely elevated (normal: 3-7d). Items are stalling in-flight; investigate blockers, dependencies, or work batch size.
notes.type_split: 30% bugs is healthy. However, 8 releases in one sprint is unusually high for mobile — verify these aren't hotfixes masking quality issues.

notes.completion_rate: 15% completion is critically low. 31 of 211 items finished suggests severe overcommitment or scope creep mid-sprint despite zero OOS.
notes.oos_total: Zero OOS demonstrates disciplined planning, but the massive total suggests initial scope was unrealistic for a single sprint.
notes.rollover: 170 items (81%) rolled over — an exceptional volume indicating planning breakdown. Review capacity assumptions and story sizing urgency.
notes.cycle_time: 18d median (42.8d avg) is far too long. Items are stalling in progress. Identify blockers, reduce WIP, and break down large stories.
notes.type_split: 33% bugs is within acceptable range. Focus efforts on improving cycle time and realistic sprint planning over bug ratio.

notes.completion_rate: Only 7% complete mid-sprint with 246 items—massive overcommit or misscoped sprint. Trajectory suggests severe underdelivery risk.
notes.rollover: 182 rollover items (74% of sprint) indicates chronic planning failure. This volume of carryforward work is unsustainable and masks true capacity.
notes.cycle_time: 65d median cycle time is catastrophic—work sitting in progress for 2+ months. Immediate intervention needed on WIP limits and blockers.
notes.type_split: 28% bugs is acceptable, but with 246 total items and 65d cycle time, the team is drowning in volume rather than executing.

notes.total: 500 tickets in scope is high for a mobile team; with 213 still in progress and 22 days remaining, scope creep is a concern and may indicate over-commitment for the quarter.
notes.completed: 217 of 500 tickets completed (43%) at 77% through the quarter. Story points show 92 of 291 delivered — tracking below expected pace with 21 days remaining. Sprint 38 delivered 85 SP; subsequent sprints have declined sharply (1-2 SP per sprint in recent sprints).
notes.completion_rate: At 43% completion with three weeks left, the team is significantly off-track to meet quarter commitments. The deteriorating sprint-on-sprint completion trend (61% → 42% → 13% → 13%) indicates systemic issues beyond normal variance.
notes.releases_shipped: 39 releases shipped across 4 closed sprints — strong continuous delivery cadence appropriate for a mobile team. Median 8 releases per sprint demonstrates consistent deployment rhythm.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint tickets is excellent sprint discipline; no reactive work has disrupted planned commitments this quarter.
notes.oos_open: No open out-of-sprint items — sprint hygiene is clean with no unplanned debt carried forward.
notes.type_split: 39% of tickets are bugs (197 of 500), signalling quality concerns that need attention; healthy mix should be <30% bugs with more focus on feature development.
notes.avg_releases: Median 8 releases per sprint is healthy for a mobile team. No concerns — frequency indicates good release discipline despite low completion rates.
notes.time_logged: 1989 hours logged across the quarter is reasonable for team size, though without estimates it's difficult to assess capacity utilisation or identify over/underloading patterns.
notes.estimate_accuracy: Zero estimation data (100% of tickets have no estimates) means planning reliability is unmeasurable; team cannot track velocity, forecast completion, or improve estimation practices without this foundational data.
notes.no_estimate: 67% of tickets (335/500) lack story point estimates, making sprint planning and capacity forecasting unreliable. This is a critical hygiene issue that undermines the team's ability to predict or manage delivery — immediate estimation discipline is required.
notes.rollover: 605 tickets rolled over across sprints (121% of quarter scope) — a severe planning and capacity issue. Sprint 40 saw 96% rollover, Sprint 39 56%, and Sprint 42 74%, indicating chronic overcommitment with no course correction sprint-on-sprint.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 13 days is reasonable for mobile development, but the mean of 25 days indicates some tickets are stalling significantly. Active sprint shows 65-day median cycle time — flag for immediate attention.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload heavily concentrated: Guido carries 27% of tickets (133 total, 61 SP planned but only 1 SP completed). Martina has 58 tickets with zero completions and minimal time logged — blocked or reassignment needed. Giovanni shows strongest delivery (31 SP completed). Several team members (Nicolas, Francesco, Daniele) show zero progress with significant SP assigned.
notes.sp_velocity: Team velocity is 43 SP/sprint across closed sprints, but actual SP completed is critically low: only 92 out of 291 planned (32%) with 22 days remaining. Sprint 40 and 41 saw near-zero SP delivery (1-2 SP each), signaling severe execution issues that have not recovered in the active sprint.
